---
title: "genepicoloc"
subtitle: "Scalable genetic colocalization for kidney genomics"
---

## Overview

**genepicoloc** provides a scalable pipeline for genetic colocalization analysis across molecular and clinical trait datasets, with a focus on kidney disease genetics.

### Key Features

- **Standalone coloc** - built-in ABF colocalization, no external dependencies
- **Preprocessing** - liftOver, variant naming, p-value underflow handling
- **Scalable pipeline** - parallel processing for millions of tests
- **Public data integration** - eQTL Catalogue, GWAS Catalog

## Kidney Genomics Atlas

Using CKDGen round 4 eGFR GWAS against 11 molecular and clinical datasets:

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Genomic regions | 259 |
| Datasets tested | 15,930 |
| Total tests | 4.1 million |
| High-confidence colocalizations | 6,439 |

See the [Kidney Atlas](atlas.qmd) for interactive exploration.

## Installation

```r
remotes::install_github("genepi-freiburg/genepicoloc")
```

See [Getting Started](vignettes/genepicoloc-workflow.qmd) for the full tutorial.

## Citation

Borisov et al. (2026) genepicoloc: Scalable genetic colocalization analysis for kidney genomics. *In preparation.*

## Contact

Institute of Epidemiology and Prevention, Faculty of Medicine and Medical Center, University of Freiburg, Germany
Hugstetter Straße 49, 79106 Freiburg
